parse.text:
Request for Proposal (RFP): Enterprise CRM
 System Development
1. Purpose
The Customer invites qualified vendors to design, develop, integrate, deploy and
support an enterprise Customer Relationship Management (CRM) platform.
2. Business Objectives
Provide a single source of truth for customers, sales pipeline, marketing, customer
service, contracts, and analytics. Improve sales conversion, automate workflows, and
provide management reporting.
3. Scope of Work
Discovery, business analysis, UX/UI, architecture, development, testing, deployment,
training, documentation, hypercare, and warranty.
4. Functional Requirements
Customer management; Leads; Opportunities; Sales pipeline; Quotes; Contracts;
Activities; Tasks; Calendar; Email integration; Marketing campaigns; Customer support;
Knowledge base; Dashboards; Role-based access; Audit logs; Notifications; Workflow
automation; REST APIs; Import/export; Multi-language; Multi-currency.
5. Non-Functional Requirements
Availability 99.9%; scalable architecture; response time <2 seconds for standard
operations; GDPR compliance; encryption in transit and at rest; SSO (OAuth2/SAML);
logging; monitoring; backup & disaster recovery.
6. Integrations
ERP, Email (Microsoft 365/Google), Telephony, BI, Identity Provider, Payment systems,
Document Management, APIs.
7. Deliverables
Requirements specification, architecture, UX/UI, source code, test cases, deployment
scripts, user guide, admin guide, training materials.
8. Project Plan
Discovery, Design, MVP, Full Implementation, UAT, Go-Live, Hypercare.
9. Vendor Response

Company profile, relevant experience, architecture proposal, implementation
approach, team, timeline, assumptions, risks, cost breakdown, support model,
references.
10. Evaluation Criteria
Functional fit 30%, Technical approach 20%, Experience 15%, Team 10%, Delivery
methodology 10%, Cost 10%, Support 5%.
11. Acceptance Criteria
All mandatory requirements implemented, UAT passed, documentation delivered,
production deployment completed, knowledge transfer finished.
12. Commercial Terms
Fixed Price preferred; milestone payments; warranty 3 months minimum; SLA proposal
required.
13. Submission
Proposal should include schedule, WBS, architecture diagrams, staffing plan, risks,
assumptions, exclusions, and commercial proposal.
